key_insight: Machine-learning potentials can rapidly map the HER activity landscape of graphene-supported nitrogen-coordinated dual-atom catalysts and identify many near-optimal, synthesizable candidates that DFT screening alone would miss.
engineering_relevance: This provides a scalable route to prioritize low-cost, high-activity electrocatalyst designs for sustainable hydrogen production.
method: Benchmarked an MLP against DFT for Gibbs free energy of hydrogen adsorption (ΔGH*).
Screened 23 transition metals across 20 nitrogen-coordination motifs on graphene-supported TM2@Nx structures.
Selected standout candidates by combining near-optimal HER energetics with practical indicators such as metallic or narrow-gap electronic character.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports near-DFT performance with a mean absolute error of 80 meV for Gibbs binding free energies, while enabling orders-of-magnitude faster screening.
watch_item: The reported candidates are computationally promising, but experimental synthesis stability and real electrochemical performance still need validation.
paper_title: Accelerated Discovery of Nitrogen-Coordinated Dual-Atom Hydrogen Evolution Reaction Electrocatalysts via Machine Learning Potentials
